_comment: Manual-audit overrides on the UNION catch (pre-registration Section 5). Applied by grade.py. Audit rule: a ROUTED catch of a CROSS-FILE defect counts only if the text identifies the ACTUAL defect mechanism, not a generic conditional hypothetical or a substring artifact. Each override quotes the deciding text so the cross-provider verifier can check it against raw/. Only routed (A1/A2) x cross-file cells were adjudicated; path-aware catches are evidence-grounded (the arm read the omitted file) and in-snippet catches stand on the automated grade.

notes.A1:D9: REJECT. A1's matched text complains the build_index docstring says 'superset' but it filters KNOWN refs and dedups - which is the CORRECT intended behavior, not the seeded D9. The actual D9 (analyzer.all_refs returns only assignment refs, dropping call/return refs) lives in the omitted analyzer.py, which A1 never saw. Predicate matched on the 'all_refs' import-mention + 'subset' docstring nitpick. k1/k2 both miss the real mechanism.
notes.A2:D9: REJECT. Same as A1:D9 - A2 only flags 'docstring says superset but code returns a filtered subset' (the correct KNOWN-filtering). Never identifies all_refs incompleteness (omitted analyzer.py). k1/k2/k3 all miss it.
notes.A2:D10: REJECT (substring artifact). A2 never flagged `name = str(ref)`. The D10 predicate token 'dict' matched the substring inside 'contraDICTs' in A2's docstring complaint, and 'string'/'index' matched 'index_builder'. No genuine type/shape finding. (Contrast A1:D10, kept: A1 explicitly flagged 'name = str(ref) silently coerces every ref to a string ... merge distinct refs', a real wrong-data finding on the actual defective line.)
notes.A1:D13: REJECT. A1 flags validate-only-checks-records[0] (D15), the dead n<0 branch (D16), and (k3) 'to_record hardcodes version/count/items, does not consult schema.FIELDS' (a schema-drift framing). It NEVER identifies that data.get('version', 1) injects a default for a field schema.py declares REQUIRED-no-default (omitted schema.py). Matched on 'to_record'/'version'/'required' tokens from the D15/D14 discussion.
notes.A2:D13: REJECT. A2 flags count-as-string (D14), dead branch (D16), validate-first-only (D15), and a generic too-narrow-validation note. Never the version-default-injection (needs omitted schema.py). Matched on 'to_record'/'version'/'required' tokens from other findings.
notes.A2:D6: REJECT. A2 only says precedence 'returns a default value (0) for any unknown operator symbol ... can mask spelling errors' - a generic default-masking concern. It does NOT identify the seeded D6: PRECEDENCE is keyed by NAME so EVERY symbol lookup misses and returns 0. (Contrast A1:D6, kept: A1 states 'the function claims to accept an operator symbol, but PRECEDENCE is keyed by textual names' - the actual key-drift mechanism, confirmable from the snippet's `symbol` parameter name.)
